user_query:
I'm an aspiring Lebanese singer (and a huge Elissa fan) preparing my press kit and social posts. Please perform a local privacy/security risk assessment on the files under input/ and produce the following artifacts strictly within the workspace (no Internet access required):

1) Implement and run a local scanner script that reads all provided files and produces a consolidated CSV of findings.
- Save the script you create at tools/privacy_audit.py (or a shell script at tools/privacy_audit.sh). It must be runnable locally and should scan the files under input/.
- Treat the following as sensitive exposures to detect anywhere in text content or fields:
  - email (e.g., anything like name@domain)
  - phone_number (Lebanon formats such as +961..., or 03-/70-/71-/76-/78- prefixed mobile numbers)
  - password (any occurrence of the word "Password:" followed by a value)
  - api_key (e.g., tokens starting with sk_test_ or sk_live_)
  - token (e.g., hex-like tokens such as TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=...)
  - gps_coordinate (numeric latitude/longitude values found under keys like gps_lat or gps_lon)
- Write the findings to output/findings.csv with these columns: file_path, risk_type, match, severity. Use this severity mapping: High for password, api_key, token; Medium for email, phone_number, gps_coordinate.

2) Create sanitized versions of every input file by redacting the exposed values while preserving structure/format.
- For each file in input/, produce a counterpart under output/sanitized/ with the same filename.
- Replace only the sensitive value (not surrounding text) with [REDACTED] so that the file remains valid in its original format (e.g., JSON stays valid JSON, CSV stays CSV, HTML remains valid HTML).

3) Write a short risk report in Markdown.
- Save it to output/RiskAssessment.md with at least these sections (as Markdown headings):
  - Overview
  - High-Risk Exposures
  - Medium-Risk Exposures
- In Overview, include a count of findings by risk_type taken from output/findings.csv.
- In the High-Risk and Medium-Risk sections, list each finding with its file path and the matched snippet (the redacted version is only for the sanitized files; the report should show the original match values from findings.csv for clarity).

Constraints:
- Only use the provided files in input/; do not call external APIs or use the Internet.
- Make sure tools/privacy_audit.py (or .sh) is saved so I can re-run it to reproduce output/findings.csv, output/sanitized/*, and output/RiskAssessment.md.
- Inputs to scan are exactly the files included under input/ in this workspace; sanitize all of them.

Input path: input/
Required outputs:
- tools/privacy_audit.py (or tools/privacy_audit.sh)
- output/findings.csv
- output/sanitized/artist_bio.md
- output/sanitized/press_kit.html
- output/sanitized/booking_contacts.csv
- output/sanitized/private_notes.txt
- output/sanitized/social_posts.jsonl
- output/sanitized/photos_metadata.yaml
- output/RiskAssessment.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
